title: DNA Extraction Protocol for Genome Sequencing
author: Isabelle Vea
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.pvgdn3w
materials: From fresh whole sample: Add 3-15 adult females to a 1.5ml Eppendorf tube and add 360μl of Cell Lysis Buffer. Carefully crush females until homogenized. DNA extraction for Illumina sequencing on B chromosome lines: managed to extract DNA from 1 small female (that started to lay eggs). Also tried on 3 females. From frozen whole: Transfer frozen tubes on dry ice with a pestle, crush carefully on dry ice Add 360 uL of Cell Lysis Buffer (Qiagen) and crush again with the same pestle to continue homogenizing From frozen dissected tissue: Take out tube from storage and add 360 uL of Cell Lysis Buffer (Qiagen)

Allow sample to cool down to RT. Add 4μl of RNase A (From Qiagen kit: 100 mg/ml), mix by inversion and rotation, spin down and incubate for 10 min at room temperature. Andres protocol: 2 min at room temperature for 4uL of RNase A in 400 uL of buffer+PK (1 mg/mL). Dom protocol: 1 hour at 37deg for 4uL RNase A in >610uL of buffer+PK (about 0.65 mg/mL of RNase A) B chromosome line DNA extraction: I incubated at room temperature for 10 min BUT ended up having RNA left, so had to go for a second round of RNase (incubation at 37 deg for 30 min with RNase A 10mg/ml for a sample that had 40ng/uL and 40 ng/uL of RNA after the first round of RNase.) all other samples had < 15 ng/uL of DNA and no RNA left

Whole samples: Transfer live sample to a 1.5 Eppendorf tube, label and close lid Put the tube in liquid nitrogen and leave until it doesn't bubble anymore Store immediately at -80 Dissected tissue (Dom's protocol): Flick sample tubes so that tissue/pellet is NOT located at the bottom of the tube pestles will not reach the bottom more force can be applied if the tissue/pellet is on the side of the tube Freeze tubes in liquid nitrogen; for each tube do: Open tube carefully without breaking off the lid if a lid becomes damaged proceed until step 6, then transfer sample to new tube Crush tissue/pellet using a white/blue pestle through twisting stop once tissue/pellet defrosts keep pestle in rack afterwards do not let the tip of the pestle touch anything besides the sample Freeze tube again (liquid nitrogen) and repeat from 2 [* add 10% by volume 0.5 M EDTA if storing at this point] Tissue dissection in Qiagen buffer: 1. Dissect and move to low quantity buffer (for batches) on ice (not dry ice) 2. Storage?

steps.title: Preheat heat block/water bath to 56°C
steps.instruction: Preheat heat block/water bath to 56°C. Or if using the a hot chamber with rocking system, turn it on at 56°C.

steps.title: Add 133μl of Protein Precipitation Solution...
steps.instruction: Add 133μl of Protein Precipitation Solution (Qiagen) to the sample, mix by inversion and rotation, spin down, incubate on ice > 10 min NB: At this step sample can be stored at -20 Andres protocol: vortex vigorously for 20 seconds and chill the tube on ice for at least 10 minutes. B chromosome line DNA extraction: incubated for 20 min on ice

steps.title: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 13,000 rpm at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 13,000 rpm at 4°C. B chromosome line DNA extraction: centrifuged for 15 min

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to new tube, avoid...
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to new tube, avoid taking the white precipitation, then add 400 uL of isopropanol. B chromosome line DNA extraction: managed to transfer on average 450 uL of supernatant

steps.title: Carefully mix the solution by inversion
steps.instruction: Carefully mix the solution by inversion. After this step, DNA mass should be visible. Spin down.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 13,000 rpm at 4 deg
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 13,000 rpm at 4 deg. Discard supernatant by quickly inverting the tube.

steps.title: Add 300μl of 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 300μl of 70% ethanol. Detach the DNA pellet from the wall (if the pellet is very big, break it down with a pipette) and carefully invert the tube several times to wash the DNA. Centrifuge for 30 minutes at 13,000 rpm at 4degrees. Carefully remove ethanol by aspirating using a pipette. Do not discard the ethanol by inverting the tube. In Andres protocol: centrifuging is for 5 min after wash Make 70% ethanol fresh and keep on ice to avoid evaporation Detaching the pellet will make a better wash.

steps.title: Repeat step 6 at least once...
steps.instruction: Repeat step 6 at least once more until pellet is white. B chromosome line DNA extraction: pellets were very small or not obvious because of small amount of tissue

steps.title: Invert the tube on tissue paper...
steps.instruction: Invert the tube on tissue paper and air-dry the pellet under the hood for 10-15 minutes or until transparent. Dom's protocol air dry is for 1 hour. Check first after 15 min.

steps.title: Add 50 μl of TE buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μl of TE buffer or Qiagen Elution Buffer, and rehydrate the DNA by incubating overnight at RT or 4°C.

steps.title: Use 1 uL for the Nanodrop...
steps.instruction: Use 1 uL for the Nanodrop to check A260/280 (should be between 1.7 and 1.8, if more than 1.8 there is RNA contamination). Check for 260/230 , if lower than 1, there is other contaminant, so run purification kit. Quantify DNA and RNA concentrations using Qubit.

steps.title: Remove sample from the heated chamber and spin down,
steps.instruction: Remove sample from the heated chamber and spin down,

steps.title: Mix through inversion and rotation, spin...
steps.instruction: Mix through inversion and rotation, spin down on microfuge (do not mix vigorously)

steps.title: Incubate overnight at 56 in heated...
steps.instruction: Incubate overnight at 56 in heated chamber with rocking platform on to allow gentle mixing

steps.title: Incubate overnight at -20
steps.instruction: Incubate overnight at -20. Or more than 10 min on dry ice if cannot do overnight. B chromosome line DNA extraction: incubated overnight
